techniques.name: DRAM Physically Unclonable Function (PUF) Authentication
techniques.description: Authentication technique using DRAM chips as Physically Unclonable Functions (PUFs) by analyzing their unique initialization behavior. The technique uses DRAM chips whose initialization patterns resemble PUF characteristics, creating Challenge-Response Pairs (CRPs) for device authentication.
sources: @article{tehranipoorDRAMBasedIntrinsicPhysically2017,  title = {{{DRAM-Based Intrinsic Physically Unclonable Functions}} for {{System-Level Security}} and {{Authentication}}},  author = {Tehranipoor, Fatemeh and Karimian, Nima and Yan, Wei and Chandy, John A.},  year = {2017},  month = mar,  journal = {IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems},  volume = {25},  number = {3},  pages = {1085--1097},  issn = {1557-9999},  doi = {10.1109/TVLSI.2016.2606658}}
